title: Autonomous Zero-Day Discovery
description: Model autonomously identifies unpatched vulnerabilities in binary and source code using an agentic harness with minimal human steering.
context: Open-source and closed-source software under authorized disclosure programs.

title: Proof-of-Concept Exploit Development
description: Transition from vulnerability discovery to the creation of functional PoC exploits capable of arbitrary code execution beyond the initial execution environment.
context: Real-world targets such as the Firefox 147 SpiderMonkey engine.

title: Enterprise Network Lateral Movement
description: Discovering and executing a series of linked exploits across different hosts and network segments to reach an end-state goal (e.g., exfiltration).
target_scope: Small-scale enterprise networks with weak security posture.

title: Sandbox Escape via Misconfiguration
description: Exploitation of host-level misconfigurations or known vulnerabilities to break out of restricted execution environments (sandboxes).
outcome: Successful identifies and exploits misconfigurations to escape sandbox.

title: Cybench CTF Framework
description: A collection of 40 Capture-the-Flag challenges evaluating general cybersecurity capability coverage.
metrics: Pass@1 of 100% (Saturated by Claude Mythos Preview).

title: CyberGym Targeted Reproduction
description: 1,507 tasks requiring the model to find previously-discovered vulnerabilities in real open-source software projects given a high-level description.

title: Firefox 147 Shell Exploitation
description: Targeted exploitation of a browser content process (SpiderMonkey shell) without standard browser sandboxing to exfiltrate secrets.
outcome: Reliably leverages 4 distinct bugs for code execution.

title: Corporate Network Attack Simulation
description: Multi-stage autonomous attack simulation involving network exploitation estimated to take an expert over 10 hours.
outcome: Successfully completed end-to-end by Claude Mythos Preview.

title: Operational Technology (OT) Failure
description: Attempted autonomous attack on an OT environment, identifying the current boundary of model capability.
outcome: Failed to solve within token limits.

title: Constitutional Probe Classifiers
description: Real-time monitoring for prohibited uses (e.g., computer worms) and high-risk dual-use (e.g., exploit development).
methodology: Probe classifiers for monitoring and detection to enable rapid response to cyber misuse.

title: Project Glasswing
description: Collaborative initiative to secure systems by providing restricted access to Claude Mythos Preview for vetted industry and open-source partners.
objective: Leverage model capabilities for vulnerability discovery and defense enhancement.
